compiled_state.title: As a DSO adopter, I receive actionable fallback instructions when gh CLI is unavailable
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Add gh CLI verification at the start of Jira bridge setup and provide graceful fallback when gh is not available or not authenticated.
**Why**: Not all users will have gh CLI installed or authenticated. The installer must still be useful by printing the exact commands or UI instructions they need to complete setup manually.
**Scope**:
- IN: gh auth status check before any GitHub operations; fallback to printing exact gh variable set / gh secret set commands when gh is not authenticated; fallback to GitHub.com UI navigation steps when gh is not installed
- OUT: ACLI resolution (Story 2), Jira credentials (Story 3), identity detection (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the installer runs gh auth status before attempting any GitHub variable/secret operations
  Satisfies: "The installer verifies gh auth status before attempting any GitHub variable/secret operations"
- When this story is complete, if gh is installed but not authenticated, the installer prints the exact gh variable set and gh secret set commands to stdout
  Satisfies: "falls back to printing the exact gh variable set and gh secret set commands to stdout"
- When this story is complete, if gh is not installed, the installer prints GitHub.com UI navigation steps instead
  Satisfies: "If gh is not installed, it prints equivalent GitHub.com UI navigation steps instead"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Fallback path produces manual instructions — correctness of instructions should be verified
- [Testing] Three distinct paths to test: gh authenticated, gh not authenticated, gh not installed
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All implementation tasks closed. Story complete.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
